creative_brief: Hero image: Rachel standing in pasture at golden hour, Kempsey NSW — real farm, real person, no stock photography. Below the fold, a clean grid showing named farms with their actual locations on a simple map of NSW and SA. Earthy palette, warm greens and cream. Typography is confident but approachable — sentence case throughout. The page should feel like a letter from someone you trust, not a pitch deck.
headline: Your groceries travel 2,400km to reach you. Ours travel from the farmer who grew them.
subhead: I'm Rachel. I left acting to raise cattle in Kempsey, NSW. What I found in farming nearly broke me — and then it showed me exactly what's broken about how Australia buys food. FarmThru is the grocery store I wish existed.
hero_copy: For years I watched farming families price their meat below what it cost to raise. Not because they wanted to — because a wholesaler told them that's what it was worth. I couldn't keep watching. FarmThru cuts every middleman between farm and family. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. You order online, collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday, and take home food that's fresher, fairer, and traceable to the person who grew it. This isn't a subscription box. It's a real grocery store built on regenerative farms.

sections.heading: I know what it costs to raise an animal honestly
sections.body: At my farm in Kempsey, I raise cattle on regenerative pasture. I know the feed costs, the vet bills, the fencing that needs replacing every winter. And I know what the big chains offer farmers for that work — it's not enough. It never has been. That's why I partnered with FarmThru. They pay farmers what the food is actually worth. Not what a wholesaler's spreadsheet says. When you buy Bundarra Farm pork belly or Paris Creek bio-dynamic dairy or Collins Wild Caught salmon from the South Coast, you're not just getting better food. You're paying the real price — and it's closer to supermarket prices than you'd think, because there's no middleman taking a cut.

sections.heading: You'll know every farm by name
sections.body: FarmThru doesn't hide behind labels. We'll show you where your food comes from — that's not a slogan, it's the entire model. Eggs from Farmer Brown in NSW. Pork and fennel salami from Bundarra Farm. Seasonal wild-caught seafood from Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast. Carbon-neutral bio-dynamic dairy from Paris Creek Farms in Meadows, SA. Regenerative beef from Little Yarran Farm. Every product traces back to a real person on real land. No anonymous supply chains. No 2,400km cold trucks. Just food we'd feed our own families — because we do.

sections.heading: How collecting works — no commitment, no subscription
sections.body: Order online whenever you need groceries. Collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. That's it. No lock-in. No weekly box you didn't ask for. Order when you want, skip when you don't. The hub model is how we keep costs low — no warehouse leases, no last-mile delivery vans. It means fresher food at fairer prices for you, and fairer pay for the farmers who grew it.

sections.heading: Own a piece of the grocery store that's changing the system
sections.body: FarmThru is opening an equity crowdfunding campaign — a chance for everyday people to be part of building something different. Not just shopping with us, but owning a piece of it. If you believe farmers deserve fair pay and families deserve to know what they're eating, you can vote for change with your wallet. Join the waitlist now with a fully refundable $5 deposit to be first in when the campaign goes live. No commitment beyond that. Investing in early-stage businesses carries real risk, including the possibility of losing your entire investment. But if this is the kind of food system you want to see — be part of it.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is conducted under the relevant ASIC regulatory framework. Any investment in FarmThru carries significant risk, including the risk of losing your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future performance. This is not financial advice. You should consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances and read the offer document in full before making any investment decision. The $5 deposit to join the waitlist is fully refundable at any time.
